perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement , irrespective of termination of this Agreement , shall survive until the 18 month anniversary of this Agreement , even after the return or destruction of Confidential Information by the Recipient (the " Confidential Period "), provid ed that for any and all trade secrets of the Disclosing Party, the Confidential Period shall last for as long as such Confidential Information qualifies as a trade secret under applicable federal, state and/or local law.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the specific duration of the Confidential Period (18 months, and indefinite for trade secrets), this edit introduces ambiguity regarding the enforceability of the NDA post-termination. It contradicts the implied intention to protect the Disclosing Party's confidential information for a defined period of time. This omission undermines the purpose of Section 10 (Non-Solicitation and Non-Circumvention) since its duration is linked to the survival of the agreement, now indefinite.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipi ent and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Disclosing Party or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries). Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, the Recipient and its Representatives shall not be restricted from hiring any employee of Disclosing Party who responds to a general solicitation for employment not directed towards the Disclosing Party’s employees.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified an 18-month or indefinite (for trade secrets) Confidential Period. The changed text removes this specification, making the survival of rights and obligations indefinite after termination. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which limits non-solicitation to 12 months after termination. The changed text creates ambiguity and the possibility of an indefinitely extending non-solicitation obligation, whereas the contradicted section explicitly limits that period. Therefore, the change constitutes an Omission-In Text Contradiction as defined in the instructions, because the duration of the survival period is an 'essential language' that affects the enforceability of the contract. The changed text creates an in-text contradiction with the duration stipulated in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees t hat money damages might not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives. Therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance and injunctive and other equitable relief as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach, and the Recipient hereby waives any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond or the showing of actual monetary damages in connec tion with such claim.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient acknowledges and agrees that money damages might not be a sufficient remedy for any breach or threatened breach of this Agreement by the Recipient or its Representatives. Therefore, in addition to all other remedies available at law, the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance as a remedy for any such breach or threatened breach.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'injunctive and other equitable relief' and 'and the Recipient hereby waives any requirement for the securing or posting of any bond or the showing of actual monetary damages in connection with such claim', the remedies available to the Disclosing Party are significantly limited, which create a contradiction. While specific performance is still available, the omission of injunctive relief (a common and vital remedy in NDA breaches) weakens the protection of confidential information as it removes the ability to immediately stop the disclosure of confidential information. This contradicts Section 3 where it establishes Recipient Obligations to safeguard all Confidential Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient Obligations . The Recipient shall protect and safeguard the confidentiality of all Confidential Information with at least the same degree of care as the Recipient would protect its own confidential information, but in no event with less than a commercially reasonable degree of care; not use the Confidential Information, or permit it to be accessed or used, for any purpose other than the Purpose, including without limitation, to reverse engineer, disassemble, decompile or design around confidenti al intellectual property; not disclose any such Confidential Information to any person or entity, except to the Recipient's Representatives who need to know the Confidential Information in relation to the Purpose and are informed of the obligations hereunder and agree to abide by the same. Recipient will promptly notify the Disclosing Party of any unauthorized disclosure of Confidential Information or other breaches of this Agreement .
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits 'injunctive and other equitable relief' and the waiver of bond requirements. The explanation states that this weakens the protection of confidential information by removing the ability to immediately stop disclosure. The contradicted text outlines the Recipient's obligations to protect and safeguard confidential information. By removing injunctive relief, the Disclosing Party loses a crucial tool to enforce these obligations, creating a direct contradiction. The original text ensures more comprehensive protection, while the modified text significantly reduces the Disclosing Party's ability to prevent breaches, thus weakening Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: During the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipi ent and its Representatives shall not contact or solicit an employee of the Disclosing Party for the purpose of hiring them, solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Disclosing Party or outside of the ordinary course of business, dir ectly or indirectly contact or participate in communications with any disclosed companies, entities or persons (including each of thei r affiliates, parents or subsidiaries).
perturbation.changed_text: During the term of this Agreement, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written consent, the Recipient and its Representatives shall not solicit the business of any client, customer or licensee of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement' and limiting only to 'During the term of this Agreement', the non-solicitation clause only applies while the NDA is active. This omission significantly weakens the non-solicitation protection and introduces an internal contradiction, since section 6 mentions that the Agreement shall survive the termination of this Agreement. Now, the non-solicitation term ends upon termination while the rest of the agreement remains in effect.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effect ive Date and shall expire 18 months from the Effective Date, provided that either Party may terminate this Agreement at any time by providing written notice to the other Party. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement , irrespective of termination of this Agreement , shall survive until the 18 month anniversary of this Agreement , even after the return or destruction of Confidential Information by the Recipient (the " Confidential Period "), provid ed that for any and all trade secrets of the Disclosing Party, the Confidential Period shall last for as long as such Confidential Information qualifies as a trade secret under applicable federal, state and/or local law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the clause 'and for a period of twelve (12) months after the expiration or termination of this Agreement' from the non-solicitation agreement. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states 'each Party's rights and obligations under this Agreement, irrespective of termination of this Agreement, shall survive until the 18 month anniversary of this Agreement'. The modified text limits the non-solicitation agreement only to the term of the agreement, while the contradicted text states that the agreement survives termination for 18 months, creating a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof , and supersedes all other understandings and agreements with respect to such subject matter.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole and entire agreement of the Parties with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'and supersedes all other understandings and agreements with respect to such subject matter' it creates an in-text contradiction. Without this clause, prior agreements or understandings between the parties regarding confidential information or the transaction under consideration could potentially be argued as still being valid, thereby creating an inconsistency in what exactly constitutes the full agreement. This absence directly clashes with the intent of Section 1 (Confidential Information), which establishes that all confidential information disclosed "on or after the Effective Date" is covered. If prior agreements remain valid, they might cover information differently, or contradict the terms of this NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information . Except as set forth in Section 2 below, " Confidential Information " means all non- public, confident ial or proprietary information disclosed on or after the Effective Date, by the Disclosing Party to the Recipient or its affiliates, or to any of such Recipient's or its affiliates' employees, officers, directors, partners, shareholders, agents, attorneys, accountants , financing sources or advisors (collectively, " Representatives "), however disclosed, including, without limitation: (a) all information concerning the Disclosing Party's and its aff iliates', and their customers' and suppliers', past, present and f uture finances, customer information, supplier information, products, services, know -how, forecasts, business, marketing, development, sales and other commercial strategies; (b) source and object code, programs, drawings, the Disclosing Party's unpatented inventions, ideas, methods and discoveries, trade secrets , unpublished patent applications and other confidential intellectual property; and (c) all notes, analyses, compilations, reports, studies, samples, data, statistics, summaries, interpretations and other ma terials prepared by or for the Recipient or its Representatives that contain or derive from the foregoing, and a ny other information that would reasonably be considered non-public, confidential or proprietary given the nature of the informati on and the Par ties' businesses.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'and supersedes all other understandings and agreements with respect to such subject matter' from the original text creates a contradiction with the 'Confidential Information' section. The original text ensured that the current agreement was the sole agreement, but the changed text allows prior agreements to potentially remain valid. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text, which defines confidential information as that disclosed "on or after the Effective Date". If prior agreements are still valid, they could cover confidential information disclosed before the Effective Date or define it differently, directly contradicting the scope defined in the contradicted text.
